title: Add Swedish country support to DataExport API

_raw.title: Add Swedish country support to DataExport API
_raw.description:
Introduce a new ~~optional country query parameter~~ on the DataExport endpoints (SingleProduct, ProductsBulk, Products). When supplied as SE, the API routes responses through a new Sweden-specific code path for the affected sections; when omitted, behavior is unchanged - existing customers on the Swedish market continue to receive the current German-default output until their integrations opt in by passing the parameter. This avoids regressions for current consumers while enabling the new Swedish customer's integration.

Update:

It has been decided that we'll implement a V2.1-branch that'll add swedish paths, with a dynamic restriction list output based on the same logic used for the overview.

_raw.title: Chemsoft SDS - Flashpoint Intervals

title: Chemsoft SDS - Updates for existing products

_raw.title: Chemsoft SDS - Updates for existing products
_raw.description:
iChemistry API currently exports SDS data (SDSCom) from MultiDB for product data for products from MultiDB_Explizit that has been added/inventoried or modified after specific date in the request.

Normally Chemsoft requests products changed since 3 days before current date/time.

SDSHubProductImport tool saves the result as XML file and creates new products that currently not found in Chemsoft (Chemsoft_ExplizitDB) and it compares the product by date.

If product with same SDS date already exists, it skips the product and proceeds to next product in the XML.

This is a problem when SDS admins modifies existing product (corrections or adds missing information) and these changes are not updated in Chemsoft databases.

We need to find a way around this to enable continuous updates for existing products without creating new products (versions) in Chemsoft database (only update the data for existing version) and we also need to modify stored procedure that distributes SDS data in client databases so it doesn't create duplicate product.

title: Add Manual Approval Gate Before Re-Enabling Servers in Load Balancer

_raw.title: Add Manual Approval Gate Before Re-Enabling Servers in Load Balancer
_raw.description:
~~Add a manual approval/gate in the deployment pipeline before reintroducing each server into the load balancer rotation.~~

~~Customers currently notice deployments due to slow initial requests caused by JIT (Just-In-Time) compilation and application initialization after deployment. As a temporary mitigation, deployments should pause after each machine has been deployed, allowing manual verification and warm-up before traffic is routed back to the server.~~

Turns out it's not possible to add gates in a rolling deployment. Testing precompilation instead.

# **Updated 2026-03-31:**


See prototypes: 

[iChemistry Web App](https://www.figma.com/proto/u1bkClU6eUTrNy6gbe94gd/iChemistry-Web-App?page-id=8250%3A119698&node-id=8252-119912&viewport=64%2C154%2C0.42&t=pS977Q0vEVNNYJyq-1&scaling=min-zoom&content-scaling=fixed&starting-point-node-id=8252%3A119912)

# **Acceptance criteria:** 

**Order of the boxes:**

Row 1: Inventory, Risk assessment, Help & support

Row 2: Substitution, Organisation, Open access

**How the boxes shall behave:**

Boxes shall be disabled (grayed out) if you do not have permission or is not included in the configuration. tooltip must explain why it is disaabled (permission or configuration).


**Behaivior of the Menu/Login:**

When you login, "new welcome page is shown".

When you press Menu "new welcome page is shown".

**Welcome page/ boxes:**

Welcome page and boxes shall be displayed if they have both activated.
Then tabs is needed.

title: FD297887 - Department resets to company level and save freezes when editing safety sheet on existing activity

_raw.title: FD297887 - Department resets to company level and save freezes when editing safety sheet on existing activity
_raw.description:
Customer reports that when editing the safety sheet for an existing activity in iChemistry, the department is automatically changed from the activity’s assigned department to the root company level.

Steps to reproduce:

1. Open an existing activity linked to a specific department (e.g. *Förrådet/SÄK 2555-Snickarverkstad*).
2. Click **Edit**.
3. Click **Done** without making changes to the risk assessment → department remains correct.
4. Navigate to the **Safety Sheet editor** tab **(Requires setting SafetySheetEditorFromRA = 1)**.
5. Observe that the department is automatically changed to the company root (e.g. *Hylte Paper AB*) instead of the activity department.
6. Attempt to manually remove the company and re-select the correct department.
7. Click **Save** → application freezes and cannot complete the save operation.

Additional observations:

* The issue does not occur on all activities/products.
* Customer suspects correlation with activities where a previous administrator edited but did not sign the safety sheet.

title: FD293901: Solve root cause of restriction list data missing for random products for multiple customers

_raw.title: FD293901: Solve root cause of restriction list data missing for random products for multiple customers
_raw.description:
I've confirmed repeated incorrect states of restriction list / substance data issues. Can we monitor how common this is and make changes to CPU to cover these scenarios so that our data self-heals? Use monitoring of the issues to see that changes we make has an impact.

Facts known:

It's common that data related to why a product is on list is missing in customer systems. (references to substances)

I've seen two variations of incorrect states for this, one where the product still have references to old version of substances in the product data, but restriction list data is updated and referring to the new substances.

In the other case data is missing but for correct substances.

There's a known issue for the case where the customer uses product application and picks a product from the global database, let's see that as a separate issue. This task is about the cases where the customer have the product active in inventory.

Scenario 1:

```sql
-- Here's a query to detect one of the cases. Would be good to monitor all customer systems for this issue:
select top 10000 prod.ArtNo, prod.ModifiedDate, mainprod.ModifiedDate, *
from GetInventory(0, 10) inv
join tblMainRegArticle prod
on inv.AttID = prod.ID and inv.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
left join MultiDB..localMainRegArticle mainprod
on prod.ID = mainprod.ID and prod.ID_mdbID = mainprod.ID_mdbID
where exists (
	select *
	from MultiDB..localDocLnkCAS multi
	where multi.AttID = prod.ID and multi.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
	and not exists (
		select *
		from tblDocLnkCAS localsubst
		where localsubst.AttID = multi.AttID and localsubst.AttID_mdbID = multi.AttID_mdbID
		and localsubst.CASAttID = multi.CASAttID and localsubst.CASAttID_mdbID = multi.CASAttID_mdbID
	)
)
and not mainprod.ModifiedDate > prod.ModifiedDate -- try to exclude products that recently have been updated by SDS team and not updated locally yet.
```

Scenario 2:

```
-- Here's a query to detect one of the cases. Would be good to monitor all customer systems for this issue:
select top 10000 prod.ArtNo, prod.ModifiedDate, mainprod.ModifiedDate, *
from GetInventory(0, 10) inv
join tblMainRegArticle prod
on inv.AttID = prod.ID and inv.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
left join MultiDB..localMainRegArticle mainprod
on prod.ID = mainprod.ID and prod.ID_mdbID = mainprod.ID_mdbID
where exists (
	select *
	from MultiDB..localMainLnkLists l
	where l.AttID = prod.ID and l.AttID_mdbID = prod.ID_mdbID
	and not exists (
		select *
		from tblMainLnkLists locall
		where locall.AttID = l.AttID and locall.AttID_mdbID = l.AttID_mdbID
		and locall.ListID = l.ListID and locall.ListID_mdbID = l.ListID_mdbID
	)
)
and not mainprod.ModifiedDate > prod.ModifiedDate -- try to exclude products that recently have been updated by SDS team and not updated locally yet.
```

If needed we can discuss strategies for monitor + making tweaks/fixes and follow impact on the fixes.

PS. It might be worth it to take a look at the timing of SDS Tools and CPU to make sure we don't have an issue if SDS team makes more than one change within a CPU cycle.
